briefdescription.para: PWM pin capture captures period.
PWM pin capture captures pulse width.
PWM pin capture captures both period and pulse width.
PWM pin capture captures a single period/pulse width.
PWM pin capture captures period/pulse width continuously.
Configure the event to trigger at the end of each PWM period.
Configure the event to trigger at a fault.

detaileddescription.para: These can be used to react to error events that caused a change of behaviour of the PWM peripheral (often disabling the output). These error events can have an internal (e.g., oscillator malfunction) or external (e.g., pio) source

briefdescription.para: Configure the event to trigger at a compare/capture match.

detaileddescription.para: This fires when the counter reaches the compare value (output compare mode) or when an external edge is captured (input capture mode).

description.para: The period cell in the PWM specifier needs to be provided in nanoseconds.
However, in some applications it is more convenient to use another scale.

briefdescription.para: Specify PWM period in nanoseconds.
Specify PWM period in microseconds.
Specify PWM period in milliseconds.
Specify PWM period in seconds.
Specify PWM frequency in hertz.
Specify PWM frequency in kilohertz.

description.para: The PWM_POLARITY_* flags are used with pwm_set_cycles(), pwm_set() or pwm_configure_capture() to specify the polarity of a PWM channel.
The flags are on the lower 8bits of the pwm_flags_t

briefdescription.para: PWM pin normal polarity (active-high pulse).
PWM pin inverted polarity (active-low pulse).
Provides a type to hold PWM configuration flags.

detaileddescription.para: The lower 8 bits are used for standard flags. The upper 8 bits are reserved for SoC specific flags.

simplesect.para: PWM_CAPTURE_FLAGS.

briefdescription.para: Provides a type to hold PWM events.

simplesect.para: PWM_EVENT_TYPES.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* pwm_capture_callback_handler_t) (const struct device *dev, uint32_t channel, uint32_t period_cycles, uint32_t pulse_cycles, int status, void *user_data)
memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct device *dev, uint32_t channel, uint32_t period_cycles, uint32_t pulse_cycles, int status, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: pwm_capture_callback_handler_t

briefdescription.para: PWM capture callback handler function signature.

simplesect.para: The callback handler will be called in interrupt context.
CONFIG_PWM_CAPTURE must be selected to enable PWM capture support.

parameterdescription.para: Captured PWM period width (in clock cycles). HW specific.
Captured PWM pulse width (in clock cycles). HW specific.
Status for the PWM capture (0 if no error, negative errno otherwise. See pwm_capture_cycles() return value descriptions for details).

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* pwm_event_callback_handler_t) (const struct device *dev, struct pwm_event_callback *callback, uint32_t channel, pwm_events_t events)
memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct device *dev, struct pwm_event_callback *callback, uint32_t channel, pwm_events_t events)
memberdef.name: pwm_event_callback_handler_t

briefdescription.para: PWM event callback handler function signature.

simplesect.para: The callback handler can be called in an interrupt context.
CONFIG_PWM_EVENT must be selected to enable PWM event support.

parameterdescription.para: Original struct gpio_callback owning this handler.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint32_t channel, uint32_t period, uint32_t pulse, pwm_flags_t flags)
memberdef.name: pwm_set_cycles

briefdescription.para: Set the period and pulse width for a single PWM output.

detaileddescription.para: The PWM period and pulse width will synchronously be set to the new values without glitches in the PWM signal, but the call will not block for the change to take effect.

Not all PWM controllers support synchronous, glitch-free updates of the PWM period and pulse width. Depending on the hardware, changing the PWM period and/or pulse width may cause a glitch in the generated PWM signal.
Some multi-channel PWM controllers share the PWM period across all channels. Depending on the hardware, changing the PWM period for one channel may affect the PWM period for the other channels of the same PWM controller.
Passing 0 as pulse will cause the pin to be driven to a constant inactive level. Passing a non-zero pulse equal to period will cause the pin to be driven to a constant active level.

parameterdescription.para: Period (in clock cycles) set to the PWM. HW specific.
Pulse width (in clock cycles) set to the PWM. HW specific.

briefdescription.para: Get the clock rate (cycles per second) for a single PWM output.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the memory to store clock rate (cycles per sec). HW specific.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint32_t channel, uint32_t period, uint32_t pulse, pwm_flags_t flags)
memberdef.name: pwm_set

briefdescription.para: Set the period and pulse width in nanoseconds for a single PWM output.

simplesect.para: Utility macros such as PWM_MSEC() can be used to convert from other scales or units to nanoseconds, the units used by this function.

parameterdescription.para: Period (in nanoseconds) set to the PWM.
Pulse width (in nanoseconds) set to the PWM.
If requested period or pulse cycles are not supported.
Other negative errno code on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct pwm_dt_spec *spec, uint32_t period, uint32_t pulse)
memberdef.name: pwm_set_dt

briefdescription.para: Set the period and pulse width in nanoseconds from a struct pwm_dt_spec (with custom period).

detaileddescription.para: This is equivalent to: pwm_set(spec->dev, spec->channel, period, pulse, spec->flags) The period specified in spec is ignored. This API call can be used when the period specified in Devicetree needs to be changed at runtime.

parameterdescription.para: Period (in nanoseconds) set to the PWM.
Pulse width (in nanoseconds) set to the PWM.

simplesect.para: A value from pwm_set().

briefdescription.para: Set the period and pulse width in nanoseconds from a struct pwm_dt_spec.

detaileddescription.para:
This is equivalent to: pwm_set(spec->dev, spec->channel, spec->period, pulse, spec->flags)
spec
PWM specification from devicetree.
pulse
Pulse width (in nanoseconds) set to the PWM.
A value from pwm_set().
pwm_set_pulse_dt()

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint32_t channel, uint32_t cycles, uint64_t *usec)
memberdef.name: pwm_cycles_to_usec

briefdescription.para: Convert from PWM cycles to microseconds.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the memory to store calculated usec.
Other negative errno code on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint32_t channel, uint32_t cycles, uint64_t *nsec)
memberdef.name: pwm_cycles_to_nsec

briefdescription.para: Convert from PWM cycles to nanoseconds.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the memory to store the calculated nsec.
Other negative errno code on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint32_t channel, pwm_flags_t flags, pwm_capture_callback_handler_t cb, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: pwm_configure_capture

briefdescription.para: Configure PWM period/pulse width capture for a single PWM input.

detaileddescription.para: After configuring PWM capture using this function, the capture can be enabled/disabled using pwm_enable_capture() and pwm_disable_capture().

simplesect.para: This API function cannot be invoked from user space due to the use of a function callback. In user space, one of the simpler API functions (pwm_capture_cycles(), pwm_capture_usec(), or pwm_capture_nsec()) can be used instead.
Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_PWM_CAPTURE.

parameterdescription.para: Application callback handler function to be called upon capture
User data to pass to the application callback handler function
if invalid function parameters were given
if PWM capture is not supported or the given flags are not supported
if IO error occurred while configuring
if PWM capture is already in progress

briefdescription.para: Enable PWM period/pulse width capture for a single PWM input.

detaileddescription.para: The PWM pin must be configured using pwm_configure_capture() prior to calling this function.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_PWM_CAPTURE must be selected for this function to be available.

parameterdescription.para: if invalid function parameters were given
if PWM capture is not supported
if IO error occurred while enabling PWM capture
if PWM capture is already in progress

briefdescription.para: Disable PWM period/pulse width capture for a single PWM input.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_PWM_CAPTURE.

parameterdescription.para: if invalid function parameters were given
if PWM capture is not supported
if IO error occurred while disabling PWM capture

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint32_t channel, pwm_flags_t flags, uint32_t *period, uint32_t *pulse, k_timeout_t timeout)
memberdef.name: pwm_capture_cycles

briefdescription.para: Capture a single PWM period/pulse width in clock cycles for a single PWM input.

detaileddescription.para: This API function wraps calls to pwm_configure_capture(), pwm_enable_capture(), and pwm_disable_capture() and passes the capture result to the caller. The function is blocking until either the PWM capture is completed or a timeout occurs.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_PWM_CAPTURE.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the memory to store the captured PWM period width (in clock cycles). HW specific.
Pointer to the memory to store the captured PWM pulse width (in clock cycles). HW specific.
Waiting period for the capture to complete.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint32_t channel, pwm_flags_t flags, uint64_t *period, uint64_t *pulse, k_timeout_t timeout)
memberdef.name: pwm_capture_usec

briefdescription.para: Capture a single PWM period/pulse width in microseconds for a single PWM input.

detaileddescription.para: This API function wraps calls to pwm_capture_cycles() and pwm_cycles_to_usec() and passes the capture result to the caller. The function is blocking until either the PWM capture is completed or a timeout occurs.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_PWM_CAPTURE.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the memory to store the captured PWM period width (in usec).
Pointer to the memory to store the captured PWM pulse width (in usec).
Waiting period for the capture to complete.
Other negative errno code on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint32_t channel, pwm_flags_t flags, uint64_t *period, uint64_t *pulse, k_timeout_t timeout)
memberdef.name: pwm_capture_nsec

briefdescription.para: Capture a single PWM period/pulse width in nanoseconds for a single PWM input.

detaileddescription.para: This API function wraps calls to pwm_capture_cycles() and pwm_cycles_to_nsec() and passes the capture result to the caller. The function is blocking until either the PWM capture is completed or a timeout occurs.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_PWM_CAPTURE.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the memory to store the captured PWM period width (in nsec).
Pointer to the memory to store the captured PWM pulse width (in nsec).
Waiting period for the capture to complete.
Other negative errno code on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct pwm_event_callback *callback, pwm_event_callback_handler_t handler, uint32_t channel, pwm_events_t event_mask)
memberdef.name: pwm_init_event_callback

briefdescription.para: Helper to initialize a struct pwm_event_callback properly.

parameterdescription.para: A valid application's callback structure pointer.
A bit mask of relevant events for the handler.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_PWM_EVENT.

briefdescription.para: Add an application event callback.

simplesect.para: As many callbacks as needed can be added on a PWM device instance.

parameterdescription.para: A valid applications callback structure pointer.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_PWM_EVENT.

parameterdescription.para: if driver does not manage event callbacks.

briefdescription.para: Remove an application event callback.

parameterdescription.para: A valid applications callback structure pointer.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_PWM_EVENT.

parameterdescription.para: if driver does not manage event callbacks.

briefdescription.para: Validate that the PWM device is ready.

parameterdescription.para: If the PWM device is ready for use
If the PWM device is not ready for use

memberdef.name: PWM_DT_SPEC_GET_BY_NAME
memberdef.initializer: { \ .dev = DEVICE_DT_GET(DT_PWMS_CTLR_BY_NAME(node_id, name)), \ .channel = DT_PWMS_CHANNEL_BY_NAME(node_id, name), \ .period = DT_PWMS_PERIOD_BY_NAME(node_id, name), \ .flags = DT_PWMS_FLAGS_BY_NAME(node_id, name), \ }

briefdescription.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec.

detaileddescription.para: This returns a static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec given a devicetree node identifier and an index.
Example devicetree fragment:
Example usage:

The device (dev) must still be checked for readiness, e.g. using device_is_ready(). It is an error to use this macro unless the node exists, has the 'pwms' property, and that 'pwms' property specifies a PWM controller, a channel, a period in nanoseconds and optionally flags.

parameterdescription.para: Lowercase-and-underscores name of a pwms element as defined by the node's pwm-names property.

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec for the property.

briefdescription.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec from a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance.

parameterdescription.para: Lowercase-and-underscores name of a pwms element as defined by the node's pwm-names property.

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec for the property.

briefdescription.para: Like PWM_DT_SPEC_GET_BY_NAME(), with a fallback to a default value.

detaileddescription.para: If the devicetree node identifier 'node_id' refers to a node with a property 'pwms', this expands to PWM_DT_SPEC_GET_BY_NAME(node_id, name). The default_value parameter is not expanded in this case. Otherwise, this expands to default_value.

parameterdescription.para: Lowercase-and-underscores name of a pwms element as defined by the node's pwm-names property

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec for the property, or default_value if the node or property do not exist.

briefdescription.para: Like PWM_DT_SPEC_INST_GET_BY_NAME(), with a fallback to a default value.

parameterdescription.para: Lowercase-and-underscores name of a pwms element as defined by the node's pwm-names property.

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec for the property, or default_value if the node or property do not exist.

memberdef.name: PWM_DT_SPEC_GET_BY_IDX
memberdef.initializer: { \ .dev = DEVICE_DT_GET(DT_PWMS_CTLR_BY_IDX(node_id, idx)), \ .channel = DT_PWMS_CHANNEL_BY_IDX(node_id, idx), \ .period = DT_PWMS_PERIOD_BY_IDX(node_id, idx), \ .flags = DT_PWMS_FLAGS_BY_IDX(node_id, idx), \ }

briefdescription.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec.

detaileddescription.para: This returns a static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec given a devicetree node identifier and an index.
Example devicetree fragment:
Example usage:

The device (dev) must still be checked for readiness, e.g. using device_is_ready(). It is an error to use this macro unless the node exists, has the 'pwms' property, and that 'pwms' property specifies a PWM controller, a channel, a period in nanoseconds and optionally flags.

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec for the property.

briefdescription.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec from a DT_DRV_COMPAT instance.

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec for the property.

briefdescription.para: Like PWM_DT_SPEC_GET_BY_IDX(), with a fallback to a default value.

detaileddescription.para: If the devicetree node identifier 'node_id' refers to a node with a property 'pwms', this expands to PWM_DT_SPEC_GET_BY_IDX(node_id, idx). The default_value parameter is not expanded in this case. Otherwise, this expands to default_value.

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec for the property, or default_value if the node or property do not exist.

briefdescription.para: Like PWM_DT_SPEC_INST_GET_BY_IDX(), with a fallback to a default value.

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec for the property, or default_value if the node or property do not exist.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to PWM_DT_SPEC_GET_BY_IDX(node_id, 0).

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec for the property.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to PWM_DT_SPEC_INST_GET_BY_IDX(inst, 0).

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec for the property.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to PWM_DT_SPEC_GET_BY_IDX_OR(node_id, 0, default_value).

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec for the property.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to PWM_DT_SPEC_INST_GET_BY_IDX_OR(inst, 0, default_value).

simplesect.para: Static initializer for a struct pwm_dt_spec for the property.

briefdescription.para: Interfaces for Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) controllers.
